misc.title: #ONEPIECE1182 Imu ring> eye symbole Zaza ring> water symbole Luffy true awakening will gain him a ring with sun/fire symbole too. Every God probably has his own unique ring.

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1182 It seems that every “GOD” has their own unique musical rhythm. The rhythm of ☀️ Sun God Nika (Monkey D. Luffy's heartbeat in Gear 5), known as the “Drums of Liberation,” is: “ドッドットット” 🎵
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1182 It seems that every “GOD” has their own unique musical rhythm. The rhythm of ☀️ Sun God Nika (Monkey D. Luffy's heartbeat in Gear 5), known as the “Drums of Liberation,” is: “ドッドットット” 🎵🎶 (DODDOTTOTTO 🎵🎶) Meanwhile, in the latest chapter, the rhythm of 🌧️Rain God Zaza is: “ドンチャンシャンシャン” 🎵🎶 (DONCHANSHANSHAN 🎵🎶) This SFX is a combination of “DONCHAN,” the sound of merrymaking or a festive atmosphere, and “SHANSHAN,” the Japanese onomatopoeia for jingling sounds. Could this be the musical counterpart to the “Drums of Liberation” 🥁 of Sun God Nika? If you look closely at Zaza’s aura ring and hat, there seem to be chimes or bells attached to them. “The Bell/Chime of Liberation,” perhaps?! 👀☕️

misc.title: Killingham: “We were born in a high place, so the idea of water falling from the sky terrifies us.” There’s something off about that explanation... In an earlier explanation from Nami, the Red Line wa
misc.text: Killingham: “We were born in a high place, so the idea of water falling from the sky terrifies us.” There’s something off about that explanation... In an earlier explanation from Nami, the Red Line was described as a continuous chain of connected islands, with winter islands and spring islands existing there as well. And since we’ve actually seen "snow" falling there, it doesn’t seem like the Red Line itself is a place where rain never falls. But in material from Oda-Sensei included in a Weekly Shonen Jump bonus, the land around Mariejois is shown and labeled as a “赤い砂漠 Red Desert.” So the reason rain doesn’t fall where they live may simply be because it has a desert climate. In other words, maybe the logic has been subtly swapped out here: Not “we’re afraid of rain because we live in a high place,” but rather, “because the place we live has a desert climate, rain doesn’t fall there—and that’s why we’re afraid of it.” So that raises the possibility that what the Celestial Dragons fear as Zaza may actually be something they were deliberately taught to fear by someone else.

misc.text: ¡¡GRAN CAPÍTULO EL #OnePiece1182 😍😍😍!! Me alegro de que Loki Vs. Imu continúe, pero quiero que llegue ya Luffy. Grande Ragnir echándole la bronca a Imu 🤣🤣🤣 Y muy interesante la aparición de la “Deidad de la Lluvia” (雨の神) que ya fue nombrada en Skypiea hace 23 años 🤯🤯🤯 Shonen Jump (@shonenjump) When it rains, it pours.
